event_blocks.summary: The segment commences with eerie, suspenseful music, a low drone, and high-pitched ringing accompanying a shadowy close-up of a person's back. The scene shifts to a dimly lit, rustic interior where Anya, a young woman in a blue tunic holding a staff, is approached by a man in dark leather. He gently touches her face, embraces her, and asks her name, to which she replies, "Anya," followed by shared laughter. He then assigns her a dangerous task, stating, "I have something for you, and it has to be packed very carefully... it can poke him full of holes if you're quick enough." Anya confidently asserts, "I can be quick," and he instructs, "You have to work at it every day. First lesson. Stick him with the point end," to which she responds, "I know which end to use." The music intensifies as the setting changes to an outdoor grassy field where a young boy in a brown tunic is engaged in a sword fight, with clashing sounds and an aggressive male voice shouting, "I've got you, you little c*nt!" The boy falls after being struck, while a distressed female voice repeatedly cries out, "Anya! No, Anya!" The mood then becomes somber and mournful as the scene cuts to a warmly lit dining area. Anya, now dressed in black with braided hair, is seated at a table, holding a knife with a focused or distressed expression, stating, "I'm practicing." An older female voice asks, "Practicing for what? The Prince?" before shouting, "Anya, stop! He's a liar and a coward, and he'll kill..." as the segment concludes.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit setting, the Man in Dark Leather, characterized by long dark hair and a beard, tenderly caressed the face of the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, who had braided hair, reminding her of her Stark heritage and their words, "Winter's coming," before emphasizing the need for mutual protection as winter approaches. The scene transitioned to a sunlit courtyard where a more mature and resolute Young Woman confronted the Curly-Haired Man, who wielded a staff, demanding to know his identity, to which he dramatically revealed himself as Syrio Forel, her dancing master. A rapid, blurred sequence ensued, filled with the cacophony of sword and staff combat, grunts, and impacts, signifying a fierce duel. Syrio Forel then commanded the Young Woman that she would marry a high lord and rule, bearing knighted sons, but she defiantly rejected this fate, stating, "No. That's not me." Later, the Man in Dark Leather, now in a formal tunic, sat with the Young Woman, dressed in a light tunic, as they intently examined a large bundle of parchment. The Curly-Haired Man's intense voice inquired about her prayers, and upon her mention of "The old and the new," he declared with conviction, "There is only one god. And his name is Death." The narrative concluded with a close-up of the Young Woman's tense, worried face as the Man in Dark Leather touched her shoulder, followed by a shot of the Curly-Haired Man, his face contorted with emotion, delivering the powerful final words, "And there is only one thing we say to Death. Not today."

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit stone interior, a distressed young woman in a light-colored tunic and scarf faces a knight in ornate dark armor, responding with resolute defiance, "Not today," to an ominous question about the God of death. The scene shifts to a tense outdoor conversation with armored men, including a man with long hair and a beard in dark leather, amidst sounds of shouting and chaos. Transitioning to a bright, open area with a flock of black birds against a hazy sky, the young woman, now with disheveled hair and a resolute expression, is accompanied by intense orchestral music and a sharp metallic clang. In a forest, a man in a brown tunic speaks urgently to her; she identifies herself as Arya, stating she has passed out, and clarifies her lineage, rejecting the title 'my lady.' A violent moment occurs as a man in a brown tunic is struck near a cauldron, falling to the ground while another in a green tunic reacts in shock. In another dimly lit interior, a bearded man in dark clothing questions a young boy about his nightmares, to which the boy replies, "I close my eyes and I see them up there, All of them." The sequence concludes with a dark, chaotic outdoor scene where a man in dark clothing is violently thrown amidst shouts and struggle, ending with a sharp impact. Later, a bald man in dark, checkered armor stands over an injured young boy in a brown tunic, remarking on a small blade. Amidst this, the young woman whispers names like "Joffrey," "Cersei," and "Ilyn Payne." In a dimly lit stone interior, a man with long, graying hair and a beard in dark leather armor commands, "Bring the girl. I need a new cupbearer." A young woman with short, dark hair in a rough, dark tunic stands before him, defiant. A knight in ornate armor interjects, and the man in dark leather interrogates her about Robb Stark, to which she replies, "They call him the Young Wolf," and asserts, "Anyone can be killed." The knight later tells her, "You saved me and the two I was with. You stole three deaths from the Red God. We have to give them back. Speak three names, and the man will do the rest." Peering through a stone wall, the knight places a protective hand on her shoulder. Her young voice then shouts, "Amory Lorch!", to which the knight confirms, "The girl has named a second." The young woman, distressed and weary, is with a knight in dark, studded armor who instructs, "Remember what we do with must be done. Now!" In a darker, enclosed space, amidst sounds of struggle and torchlight, the knight's voice instructs, "If the day comes when you must find me again, just give that coin to any men from Braavos and say these words to him. Valar Morghulis." The young woman repeats, "Valar Morghulis." As the man in the rugged tunic turns, sounds of a dog barking are heard, followed by an exclamation, "Aha! Not a man at all! A HOUND!" A gruff voice shouts, "Girl! What in seven hells are you doing with this Stark bitch?" The young woman confronts the speaker, accusing, "You murdered Myca! The butcher's boy! My friend! He was 12 years old! He was unarmed! And you rode him down!" The man in the rugged tunic states, "I'm done serving," and explains his connection to his chosen family. The young woman, in a close-up, is told by the Woman in Red Hooded Cloak, "I see a darkness in you... Eyes you'll shut forever. We will meet again." The Man in Dark Leather declares, "The Red God is the one true God. You've seen his power," to which the Young Woman defiantly responds, "He's not my one true God. Death." In a daytime outdoor scene, the Knight in Ornate Armor warns the Young Woman, "You're very kind. Someday it'll get you killed," and she fiercely retorts, "I'm not afraid," and threatens, "Someday, I'm going to put a sword through your eye and out the back of your skull." Later, a frightened Young Boy in a dark tunic is surrounded by armed men as a chanting crowd shouts, "Kill the crow!" and the Young Woman pleads, "Don't kill him! Please." The final shot shows the Young Woman in the dark tunic standing defiantly in a forest, holding a small object, as the Man in Rugged Tunic asks about the "kind of coin" she holds, to which she replies, "It's worth a lot."

event_blocks.summary: In a dark, rain-soaked forest, the sounds of a violent struggle, grunts, and sword clangs accompany a man in dark leather armor standing over a fallen figure. The scene transitions to a dimly lit interior where a young woman in black, her hands blood-stained, clutches a small, round object. A bald man in checkered armor observes her, grimly inquiring, "Is that the first man you've killed?" She resolutely confirms, "The first man." As she stands over an injured man on the ground, a male antagonist's voice proposes a trade: "One of our little chickens for one of yours. Give us a go at your friend." Amidst further struggle sounds, another male voice taunts the injured man about his leg, eliciting a stuttered, confused reply. The young woman then asks the injured man, "Can you walk?" before menacingly remarking about a "fine little blade" she might "pick my teeth with." The setting shifts to an overcast outdoor landscape where a knight in ornate armor confronts the young woman. Distressed, she cries out, "He took us in! He fed us!" The knight, with a deep, authoritative voice, dismisses her, stating, "He's weak. He can't protect himself. We'll both be dead come winter. That man don't need silver." The young woman defiantly shouts back, "You're the worst shit in the Seven Kingdoms!" The knight calmly retorts, "I just understand the way things are. How many Starks they got to behead before you figure it out?" He then turns and walks away into the misty distance, his voice echoing with a final, dismissive challenge: "Go on, do it for your supposed friend. Dead like all the rest of your friends."

event_blocks.summary: The Knight in Ornate Armor, with a grim expression and gravelly voice, stands over the motionless Young Woman in Black, dagger raised, declaring her friend is dead but Meram Trant and John Tadama are not. The Young Woman in Black, now sitting in a misty, wooded area, tends to the injured Man in Rugged Tunic, stating nothing could be worse, to which he philosophically replies, "Maybe nothing is worse than this." She asserts "Nothing is just nothing," then angrily recounts a violent threat. The Man in Rugged Tunic cynically observes her day isn't going as planned and asks if "He" is on her list; she replies she doesn't know his name, revealing hers as Rogue. He thanks her, and as intense percussive sounds and metal clanging return, he encourages her, "You're learning." Rogue and the Knight in Ornate Armor stand together solemnly in the mist, the Knight placing a hand on her shoulder. Rogue then calls out, "Come with me, Arya," revealing the Man in Rugged Tunic's name, before they walk through a body-strewn battlefield. Separately, the Woman in Dark Armor stands alone in a desolate, mountainous landscape, her expression one of shock. Later, the Young Woman in Black, her face etched with sorrow, stands in a bleak, fog-laden landscape as a harsh voice lists deaths and declares Winterfell in rubble. A ghostly overlay appears as she lies injured on rocky ground, reaching for the wounded Man in Rugged Tunic. A desperate plea to be killed is heard. In a warmer, sunlit setting, the Curly-Haired Man confronts her, asking "How do you?" to which she calmly replies, "Valar Morghulis," and he responds, "Valar Dohaeris." She accuses him of lying about Jaqen H'ghar's presence, and he cryptically states he is "No one." In a dim, stone-walled interior, the Curly-Haired Man's voice explains H'ghar must become no one to serve the Many-Faced God. The Young Woman in Black walks a dark corridor, her face hardened, and the Curly-Haired Man asks whose sword it is, revealing it belongs to Arya Stark. The Woman in Dark Armor is again seen in a desolate, mountainous landscape, her armor gleaming, eyes wide with shock. The Young Woman in Blue Tunic, identified as Arya Stark by the Man in Dark Leather, stands tense in a dimly lit interior, questioned about her clothes and stolen silver. She kneels by a rocky waterfront at dusk, gripping a sword. Returning to the interior, she pleads to be killed, recounting leaving the Hound, Sandor Clegane, to die and expressing hatred. The Man in Dark Leather accuses her of lying and questions if she truly wants to be no one, to which she affirms. A sharp impact and gasp are heard, and she defiantly declares she's not playing this game anymore, as the Man in Dark Leather chillingly concludes, "We never stop playing." The Young Woman in Blue Tunic is last seen in a dim corridor, looking up with fear and defiance.

raw_segments: The sequence opens with intense, percussive music and sounds of struggle as a man in dark, studded armor, the Knight in Ornate Armor, stands over the motionless Young Woman in Black, raising a dagger. His grim, conflicted expression is underscored by his deep, gravelly voice declaring, "Your friend's dead. But Meram Trant's not. Just John Tadama. And a big fucking sword." The camera cuts to a close-up of the Young Woman in Black’s face, her eyes wide with pain and fear as the dagger hovers. The scene shifts to a misty, wooded area where the Young Woman in Black, now sitting, tends to an older man, the Man in Rugged Tunic, who is injured and bleeding. Her soft, strained but determined voice states, "Nothing could be worse than this," to which the Man in Rugged Tunic, with a weary, philosophical tone, replies, "Maybe nothing is worse than this." She continues, "Nothing isn't better or worse than anything," and "Nothing is just nothing," before angrily recounting, "He told me he'd fuck me bloody with a stick." The Man in Rugged Tunic, with a calm, cynical voice, observes, "This day's really not working out the way you planned. He on your little list?" She defiantly responds, "He can't be. I don't know his name." He then asks, "What's your name?" and she reveals, "Rogue." He replies, "Thank you." The atmosphere remains tense and somber, with muted colors and foggy lighting. Intense, percussive sounds and metal clanging return as the Man in Rugged Tunic encourages her, "You're learning." The scene transitions to a wider shot of Rogue and the Knight in Ornate Armor standing together in the mist, both looking off with solemn expressions, the Knight placing a hand on her shoulder in a gesture of camaraderie. Rogue then urgently calls out, "Come with me, Arya," revealing the Man in Rugged Tunic's name. The final shot reveals a broader battlefield, littered with bodies, as Rogue and the Knight in Ornate Armor walk through the carnage with resolute faces, before cutting to a new figure, a Woman in Dark Armor, standing alone in a desolate, mountainous landscape with an expression of shock or realization.

The sequence opens with a close-up of the Young Woman in Black, her face etched with sorrow and resolve, standing amidst a bleak, fog-laden landscape. A harsh male voice questions, "Safety! Where the fuck's that?", listing the deaths of her aunt, mother, father, and brother, and declaring "Winterfell's a pile of rubble," before concluding, "There's no safety, you dumb bitch." A ghostly overlay of a bearded man’s face, possibly a memory or vision, appears as the Young Woman in Black is then seen lying injured on rocky ground, her hand reaching out to the Man in Rugged Tunic, who is also wounded and bleeding from his arm, with a grim, cold atmosphere and mist swirling around them. A metallic clanking sound precedes a desperate female voice pleading, "Kill me! Kill me!" The scene transitions to a warmer, sunlit setting where the Curly-Haired Man, now in a rugged tunic, confronts the Young Woman in Black with intense emotion, gesturing urgently. He asks, "How do you?", to which she calmly replies, "Valar Morghulis," and he responds with "Valar Dohaeris." The Young Woman in Black then confronts him, accusing, "You said there was no Jaqen H'ghar here," to which he cryptically replies, "There isn't. A man is not Jaqen H'ghar." She presses, "Well, who are you then?", and he states, "No one. And that is what we go must become." The mood shifts to a dim, stone-walled interior where the Young Woman in Black stands before a hooded figure—the Knight in Ornate Armor—holding a staff, his expression stern and authoritative. The Curly-Haired Man's voice continues, philosophically stating, "H'ghar wants to serve herself. Yeah, we serve the Many-Faced God, to serve well. H'ghar must become no one." The Young Woman in Black then walks through a dark, torch-lit corridor, her face illuminated by flickering light, her expression hardened with determination. The Curly-Haired Man's voice then asks, "Whose sword is that?", before revealing, "It belongs to Arya Stark. Arya Stark." The final shot reveals the Woman in Dark Armor standing alone in a desolate, mountainous landscape, her polished dark armor gleaming faintly in the dim light, her eyes wide with shock or realization, suggesting she has just witnessed or discovered something pivotal. Throughout, the lighting shifts from cold, misty blues to warm, golden tones and back to deep shadows, emphasizing emotional and narrative transitions.

The minute opens in a dimly lit, shadowy interior where the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, identified by the Man in Dark Leather's deep, calm voice as "Aya Stark," stands tense as he questions her about "Aya Stark's clothes" and "stolen silver," wondering why "no one" came to be surrounded by her things. The scene briefly cuts to a close-up of a hand gripping a sword with an ornate golden hilt, then to the Young Woman in Black kneeling by a rocky waterfront at dusk, gripping the same sword with a pained or determined expression, all set against a somber, suspenseful background score. Returning to the dark interior, the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, now distressed and pleading, narrates in a slightly strained voice her encounter with "the Hound, Sandor Clegane," recounting how she left him to die in the mountains, expressing her intense hatred and desire for him to suffer. A metallic clang signals a sword being drawn, and she defiantly shouts, "I hated him!" and "That's not a lie!" The Man in Dark Leather, observing with a cold, calculating gaze, accuses her of lying to him and to the Many-Faced God, questioning if she "truly want[s] to be no one." She firmly replies, "Yes!" followed by a sharp impact sound and a gasp. With the music swelling dramatically, she defiantly declares, "I'm not playing! This stupid game anymore!" as the Man in Dark Leather, a looming presence, chillingly concludes, "We never stop playing." The Young Woman in Blue Tunic is last seen in a dim corridor, her face illuminated by flickering light, looking up with a mix of fear and defiance.

event_blocks.summary: In near darkness, a man's grim face is shown with ethereal music as a calm male voice states, "The girl is not ready to become no one. But she's ready to become someone else." The scene abruptly shifts to a dimly lit, candle-lit interior where a man in a brown tunic is violently attacked with a loud thud and pained grunt, falling to the ground as another figure strikes him. Sounds of struggle, more pained grunts, and screams intensify as the Young Woman in Blue Tunic rushes to his side, her face contorted in distress as she kneels beside him. She then stands and forcefully grabs the man’s head, holding it as he writhes in pain, his face slick with sweat and blood, illuminated by flickering candlelight, his agony punctuated by choked sounds and gasps. A cut to a dark, rainy night reveals the Woman in Red Hooded Cloak, her face partially obscured, staring intently off-screen with a cold, calculating expression, her calm, clear voice asking, "They're killing Syria for Al, remember him? Do you know who I am?" The scene returns to the dim interior, where the man continues to struggle, groaning "Oh, God..." as the hooded woman's voice asks, "Do you know who you are?" and then declares, "You're no one. You're nothing." The dramatic music swells with sounds of struggle and heavy breathing. The man in the brown tunic, still in agony, then utters with effort, "Only death can pay for life." The final shot returns to the Young Woman in Blue Tunic in the dim interior, now holding a small, dark object—possibly a vial or blade—her face illuminated by firelight, her expression one of grim determination as she looks up, seemingly confronting an unseen threat, her own heavy, distressed breathing audible. The minute opens with a wide, atmospheric shot of a dark, fog-laden courtyard flanked by towering, ancient stone pillars, establishing a foreboding and isolated setting under dim, blue-toned lighting, with the 'INFINITEX' text visible. Ominous, building music underscores the scene as the Young Woman in Black stands in the center. The scene cuts to a close-up of the Young Woman in Blue Tunic lying on the ground, her face contorted in pain and fear, her light tunic and scarf soaked and torn. She gasps and cries out, "No! No! No!", then desperately pleads, "You can't die! Don't die!" and asks, "Who is this?" A male voice, seemingly an internal thought or unseen narrator, states, "No one at all. Faces are for no one. You are still someone. And to someone, their faces are as good as poison," as the Young Woman in Blue Tunic continues to struggle and gasp. The camera then shifts to show the Young Woman in Black standing over her, expression cold and resolute, holding a wooden staff. The Young Woman in Blue Tunic attempts to rise, crying "I can't see!" and repeatedly asking "What's happening?" The Young Woman in Black strikes her with the staff, eliciting a grunt of pain. The scene transitions to a stone-walled alley where the Young Woman in Black, now wielding the staff with force, confronts the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, who is now on her feet, defiant but disoriented, again stating "I can't see." The Young Woman in Black, with a firm, cold voice, commands, "Stand and fight, blind girl," and dismissively adds, "That's your problem, not mine." The two engage in a tense, physical struggle, punctuated by grunts and sounds of impact, with the Young Woman in Black overpowering her opponent. The final shot shows the Young Woman in Black standing over the fallen Young Woman in Blue Tunic, who lies motionless on the ground, the staff resting beside her, as the scene fades into darkness. The sequence opens with a tense confrontation in a dim, overgrown outdoor setting, where the Young Woman in Black, wielding a wooden staff, aggressively engages the Young Woman in Blue Tunic amidst percussive background music and the sharp sounds of staffs clashing. The Young Woman in Blue Tunic, visibly injured and distressed, struggles to defend herself, grunting in pain and effort as she parries. The scene shifts to a dark, enclosed stone chamber, illuminated by a single beam of light, where the intense, choreographed combat continues. A deep, calm male voice asks, "Who are you?" to which the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, with a strained and defiant voice, replies, "No one." The male voice continues, stating, "If a girl says her name, a man will let her sleep under a roof tonight," as the Young Woman in Black dominates, striking with force and precision. The Young Woman in Blue Tunic defiantly repeats, "A girl has no name." The male voice persists, "If a girl says her name, a man will give her eyes back," before the Young Woman in Black eventually knocks the Young Woman in Blue Tunic to the ground, where she lies motionless, her staff beside her. The Young Woman in Blue Tunic again states, "A girl has no name." The male voice then asks, "Who were you before you came here? Are you Stark?" followed by a sharp grunt of pain from the fallen woman. The male voice continues his interrogation, asking, "Tell me about the Hound? Also dead?" as more staff impacts are heard. The victor, the Young Woman in Black, stands over her, breathing heavily, her expression cold and resolute, before turning away, her silhouette stark against the shadows. The male voice then asks, "Who else was on Arya Stark's funny little list?" prompting the Young Woman in Blue Tunic to name "Cersei Lannister," "Gregor Clegane," and "Walder Frey," before the male voice concludes, "Are you sure you're not forgetting someone?" The environment transitions from natural to claustrophobic, emphasizing the grim atmosphere.

Wielding a wooden staff, the Young Woman in Black strikes the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, who stumbles and falls, crying out amidst sounds of impacts and struggle, her agony captured as the Young Woman in Black stands over her. A voice-over states, "If a girl is truly no one, she has nothing to fear." The scene shifts to a bright, sunlit outdoor riverbank where a woman in a brown tunic and headscarf is laughing with a joyful young boy in a brown tunic, but this peace is shattered when the woman is violently struck by a staff, her face contorting in pain amid an ominous hum. She then sprints through a bustling marketplace, scattering produce and causing chaos. The sequence concludes with her scaling a stone wall and standing with staff raised in darkness. In a dimly lit dungeon with flickering candlelight and preserved heads in alcoves, a young woman with braided hair, appearing distressed, confronts an older man in dark robes who declares, "Finally, a girl is no one." She defiantly states, "A girl is Arya Stark of Winterfell," and "I'm going home." When he demands, "Where are my damn moron sons?", she chillingly replies, "Here, my lord. event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, candle-filled hall with a dramatic orchestral score, a young woman with dark hair in a dark, fur-lined tunic contemplates, as a man with curly dark hair in a rugged tunic urgently speaks to her. She declares, "When people ask you what happened here, tell them the North remembers. Tell them Winter came for House Frey." He informs her that "Jon Snow came down from Castle Black with a wildling army and won the Battle of the Bastards. He's King of the North now." She sharply interjects, "You're lying," and he questions, "Why would I lie about that? He's your brother, right?" The scene abruptly shifts to a cold, snowy forest at twilight with intensifying music and growling sounds. The young woman, now in a heavy fur-lined cloak, stands before a pack of large, gray wolves, raising a staff with a determined yet fearful expression, her breath visible. The wolves circle cautiously, one baring its teeth and snarling, its eyes locked on her amidst deep guttural growls. She whispers, "Nymeria?" A powerful wolf howl is heard, followed by more growls. She whispers again, "Nymeria, it's me." A softer wolf sound prompts her response, "Oh, yeah." raw_segments: The minute opens in near-total darkness, accompanied by ominous, suspenseful orchestral music with a low, sustained drone and the sounds of wind, as a figure moves cautiously through a snowy, desolate landscape, illuminated only by faint, flickering light sources. The scene transitions to a wide, chaotic battlefield at night, where numerous motionless figures lie in the snow and a few survivors huddle around a central fire, with distant clanging and muffled shouts suggesting a recent, brutal conflict. The ominous music intensifies with a rising crescendo before softening as the scene cuts to a dimly lit interior, possibly a hall or keep. Here, a Woman with Pale Hair stands at a candle-adorned table, raising a goblet in a solemn gesture amidst others who appear to be in mourning or ritual. A crowd murmurs, then erupts in a loud cheer as a clear voice exclaims, "To Arya Stark, the hero of Winterfell!" The focus then shifts to close-ups of the Young Woman in Black, her face illuminated by candlelight, her expression shifting from fear to distress, her eyes wide and tearful, as a male voice softly but intensely declares, "All I know is that you're beautiful, and I love you, and none of it will be worth anything if you're not with me." He continues, "Be my wife. Be the lady of Storm's End." The Young Woman in Black responds with a soft, slightly strained voice, "You'd be a wonderful lord. And any lady would be lucky to have you," before firmly adding, "I'm not a lady. I never have been. That's not me." The scene abruptly cuts to a different stone-walled interior where the Curly-Haired Man, with an intense expression, confronts the Young Woman in Black, who appears distressed with hands raised defensively. A gruffer male voice, likely his, menacingly states, "I'm going to kill you," then continues, "You think you wanted revenge a long time. I've been at it all my life. It's all I care about," before shouting, "Look at me! Look at me!"

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, stone-walled interior, the Curly-Haired Man, with long curly hair and a beard, wearing dark, rugged clothing, confronts the Young Woman in Black, who is clad in a heavy, fur-lined cloak with intricate lacing. His deep, resonant voice declares, "You want to be like me? Then you come with me. You die here." The Young Woman in Black whispers "Sandal" and then, with resignation, says "Thank you." A sharp struggle sound erupts, followed by her gasp and distressed cries, as the scene abruptly transitions to a chaotic, smoke-filled exterior filled with the sounds of crumbling stone, debris, and screams, set against a frantic, percussive musical score. The Young Woman in Black runs frantically through the rubble, her face contorted in fear, navigating collapsing structures, her desperate grunts and shouts punctuating the cacophony as she reaches out for help and collapses amidst the devastation. Explosions and more screams intensify, culminating in her terrified shouts of "Take my hand! Take my hand! Kill him! Kill him!" The scene ends with a wide shot of the burning, crumbling fortress, flames engulfing the background, and silhouettes of figures fleeing or falling. The minute opens with overwhelming chaos as buildings collapse amidst roaring fires and thick smoke, accompanied by loud explosions, crumbling debris, and the terrified screams and frantic shouts of a panicked crowd. A young woman with dark hair, wearing a heavy, fur-lined cloak, is seen lying injured under rubble, her face bloodied and eyes wide with shock. She struggles to rise, coughing and gasping for air as dust fills the scene. A man in rugged clothing helps her to her feet, and together they flee through the ruins, dodging falling stones and flames, their frantic movements underscored by more explosions and frantic shouts. The scene briefly shifts to a dimly lit stone interior where a group huddles, illuminated by flickering candlelight, their fear palpable, as a somber, orchestral score plays. Returning to the exterior, the chaos intensifies with renewed explosions and frantic sounds of struggle. The young woman, now standing, urgently declares, "If you stay here, you'll die," her voice determined. She then repeatedly commands, "Follow me!" as she runs through the smoke with a child in her arms, guided by the man. Amidst more shouts and the sounds of struggle, she urgently cries, "Get up! Get up!" before a final, prolonged, pained scream echoes through the devastation, emphasizing the relentless danger and desperate urgency of their escape.

event_blocks.summary: In a rugged, rocky outdoor setting, the Curly-Haired Man questioned the Young Woman in Black, "Caswall," asking if anyone would dare tell her women weren't allowed, to which she resolutely replied, "I can't. I'm not going back north." The Red-Haired Woman in Fur-Collared Cloak and the Young Boy in Fur-Lined Cloak observed with concern. Caswall then asked the Curly-Haired Man about the destination west of Westros, and upon his admission of ignorance, declared her intention to go there, stating, "No one knows. It's where all the maps stop. That's where I'm going." A somber orchestral score accompanied this dialogue. The scene shifted to a dimly lit interior with a silhouetted figure walking down a shadowy corridor towards a bright doorway, before cutting to Caswall alone on the deck of the Ship with Dragon Prow, gazing at the vast, gray sea under a cloudy sky, her posture resolute as the ship's dragon-headed prow was shown. Later, Caswall, with dark hair pulled back and wearing a heavy, fur-lined cloak over detailed dark armor, stood resolutely on the ship's deck, her face etched with determination and solemnity, confronting an unseen challenge under a cloudy, overcast sky, the camera emphasizing her stillness and the somber, tense atmosphere as the Ship with Dragon Prow sailed through calm but foreboding waters in cool, muted lighting.
